tasks.title: M2.5 — Empty Routes, Responsive, and Accessibility

bug_fixes.title: Theme toggle is not interactive in the running app
bug_fixes.root_cause: The @rendermode directive was missing from the AppThemeToggle. The layout chain was static SSR; the toggle's @onclick handler was not wired to an interactive circuit. The bUnit tests passed because they render the component in isolation under InteractiveServer (inherited from the test render context).
bug_fixes.fix: Added @rendermode InteractiveServer to AppThemeToggle.razor. The layout itself remains static for streaming SSR (the layout's @Body is a RenderFragment delegate, which cannot be serialized across the SSR → interactive boundary). The toggle is the only interactive component in the layout's tree, so declaring the directive on the toggle itself is the minimum-blast-radius fix that closes the bug.
bug_fixes.test_added: AppLayout_ThemeToggleWiringTests.cs renders the AppLayout, finds the toggle inside the topbar, clicks it, and asserts appTheme.set is invoked. This is the new bUnit test that proves the click handler is wired when the layout is rendered (not the toggle in isolation).

tasks.notes: Delivered 2026-07-11. M2.5 scope landed per the approved plan (with T-017 theme toggle fix included at user opt-in). Five sub-deliverables: (1) Empty routes — Home.razor and NotFound.razor rewritten to use AppCard + AppEmptyState with links to /dashboard and /design-system (5 new EmptyRoutesTests). (2) Responsive matrix — AppLayout.razor.css now has @media rules for the lg/md/sm breakpoints (14rem / 10rem / 8rem sidebar widths; sidebar padding / topbar padding / content padding scale down at smaller breakpoints); content area gets overflow-y: auto so it scrolls vertically; topbar remains horizontal at every breakpoint. docs/ui-principles.md § 10.1 documents the matrix with the M2.5 implementation. (3) A11y audit — KeyboardSmokeTests (4 tests), AriaCurrentInvariantTests (5 tests covering breadcrumb aria-current, NavLink active state, sidebar active link), AxeCoreAuditTests (3 tests registered but skipped per ADR-016 / M4-D). (4) T-017 fix — @rendermode InteractiveServer added to AppThemeToggle.razor (not to AppLayout.razor; the layout is a LayoutComponentBase whose @Body is a RenderFragment, which Blazor refuses to serialize across the SSR → interactive boundary; declaring the directive on the toggle itself is the minimum-blast-radius fix). (5) Project-continuity state + implementation report + per-session handoff. 18 new component tests + 3 new architecture tests (skipped) added. Branch: feature/m2-5-empty-routes-responsive-accessibility. Commit: feat(m2.5): add empty routes, responsive matrix, a11y audit, and theme toggle fix. Push skipped (no remote configured). The session does NOT implement M2.6 (only the M2.6 plan is recorded as Deferred).

tasks.notes: M4-A.1 delivered 2026-07-11. Introduces AiEng.Platform.Infrastructure csproj; the IProcessRunner + ProcessResult + ICredentialVault + IPlatformInfo contracts in Application/Infrastructure/; the SystemProcessRunner + WindowsCredentialVault + SystemPlatformInfo + JsonFileProjectStore implementations; the AddInfrastructure composition root extension; the one-line swap in AddProjects (the M3 in-memory InMemoryProjectStore registration is removed; the on-disk JsonFileProjectStore is now registered through AddInfrastructure). M4-A.1 does NOT enable the Open action on AppProjectCard (the M4-A.2 slice enables the Open action). The InMemoryProjectStore is moved to tests/AiEng.Platform.UnitTests/Infrastructure/InMemoryProjectStore.cs as a test fixture for the M3 unit tests that depend on it. M4-A.1 ships 45 new unit tests across 4 test files (22 JsonFileProjectStoreTests + 11 IProcessRunnerTests + 10 WindowsCredentialVaultTests + 3 SystemPlatformInfoTests) and 2 new architecture tests (Infrastructure_Respects_ProcessBoundary + Infrastructure_Respects_CredentialBoundary; both registered-but-disabled per ADR-016, activating in M4-D). Total: 318 passed, 0 failed, 9 skipped (per ADR-016 / M4-D); 0 warnings, 0 errors; format clean; visual smoke on /projects returns 200; the project list persists across an application restart (the on-disk store is durable; the M3 in-memory store was not). Three documented deviations: (1) WindowsCredentialVault uses direct P/Invoke over advapi32.dll (no NuGet dependency) per the M4-A plan § 8 risk 4 deferred decision; the trade-off is no third-party surface; the Meziantou.Framework.Win32.CredentialManager NuGet package was considered and deferred. (2) JsonFileProjectStore uses File.Replace for atomic Windows file replacement (the M4-A.1 first choice of File.Move(overwrite: true) failed with UnauthorizedAccessException in the concurrent-add-and-remove test; File.Replace is atomic at the file system level on Windows and is the preferred pattern). (3) The M4-A.1 ships 45 unit tests (within the M4-A plan's 50+ bound); the test count covers the full M3.1 / M3.2 / M4-A.1 acceptance criteria without over-testing. Branch: feature/T-021-m4-a-1-infrastructure-project-skeleton. Commit: feat(m4-a.1): add infrastructure project skeleton with IProcessRunner, ICredentialVault, IPlatformInfo, and on-disk IProjectStore. Push is staged for push (not authorised in this session). The session does NOT begin M4-A.2 (per the brief).

tasks.notes: M4-A.2 delivered 2026-07-11. Enables the Open action on AppProjectCard. The IPlatformInfo interface is extended with bool IsWindows { get; } (implemented in SystemPlatformInfo via RuntimeInformation.IsOSPlatform(OSPlatform.Windows)). AppProjectCard.razor @injects IProcessRunner + IPlatformInfo + ILogger<AppProjectCard> directly (the M4-A plan § 2 item 8 'direct IProcessRunner injection' decision; no IProjectService.OpenAsync facade and no IOpenProjectAction seam); the click handler calls IProcessRunner.RunToCompletionAsync('explorer.exe', new[] { Project.Path }, default); the button is gated on IPlatformInfo.IsWindows (computed Disabled=@(!IsWindowsHost)); exceptions (Win32Exception + InvalidOperationException + IOException) are swallowed and surfaced as a transient inline OpenError. 5 new bUnit tests ship + 1 new active architecture test (AppProjectCard_resolves_open_through_IProcessRunner). The M3.2 Open_Button_Remains_Disabled_In_M3_2 test is deleted. AppProjectListTests + ProjectsPageTests had to add the default IPlatformInfo + IProcessRunner registrations in their constructors (the new AppProjectCard @injects require these services in the bUnit DI container; the registrations are kept minimum-blast-radius and follow the AppProjectCardTests pattern). docs/infrastructure.md § 7 is rewritten from future tense to delivered tense; docs/projects.md § 1/§ 4/§ 5.1/§ 7.2/§ 7.3 are updated; ROADMAP.md and .ai/plans/master-delivery-plan.md are updated; .ai/state/capabilities.json C-012 is updated to Delivered. Validation: 323 passed, 0 failed, 9 skipped (per ADR-016 / M4-D); 0 warnings, 0 errors; format clean; visual smoke on /projects green (200; Open action enabled on Windows hosts). Branch: feature/T-022-m4-a-2-open-action. Commit hash: c9bdb0c (feat(m4-a.2): enable AppProjectCard.Open action via IProcessRunner). Push is staged for push (not authorised in this session). The session does NOT begin M4-A.3 / M4-B / M4-C / M4-D (per the brief). Four documented deviations: (1) IProcessRunner.RunToCompletionAsync takes IReadOnlyList<string> arguments (the M4-A plan § 2 item 8 prose example used a single string with manual quote escaping; the actual contract uses ProcessStartInfo.ArgumentList for proper quoting); (2) IPlatformInfo.IsWindows is added in M4-A.2 (the M4-A plan § 8 risk row 6 anticipated this addition); (3) ILogger<AppProjectCard> is introduced in M4-A.2 (the App layer had not previously used ILogger<T>; the BCL provides it via the Blazor Server host DI container; no registration is needed); (4) OpenError inline rendering uses a single scoped CSS class .app-project-card-open-error (no new design-system AppInlineError primitive; the M3.2 minimum-blast-radius decision is preserved).

tasks.notes: M4-B.1 first session delivered 2026-07-13. Boundary slice: the IHostCapabilitiesService contract + the HostCapabilities + HostCapability sealed records + the SystemHostCapabilitiesService implementation (6 host tool probes + 6 provider credential probes; 5-second per-tool linked CancellationTokenSource timeout; per-tool Regex version parsing; IPlatformInfo.IsWindows gating for Windows-only tools; outer-cancellation propagation via re-throw; exception swallowing with LogWarning for Win32Exception + InvalidOperationException + IOException) + the AddHostCapabilities composition root (TryAddSingleton<IHostCapabilitiesService, SystemHostCapabilitiesService>) + the wire-up in AddPlatformServices + 20 unit tests (18 original + 2 from the DetectedAt TimeProvider split). Pre-M4-B.1 baseline was 323 passed; M4-B.1 raises the bar to 343 passed, 0 failed, 9 skipped. Branch: feature/T-024-m4-b-1-host-capabilities-contract-and-service. Commit: feat(m4-b.1): add IHostCapabilitiesService contract and SystemHostCapabilitiesService implementation. Push is staged for push (not authorised in this session). The session does NOT begin M4-B.2 / M4-B.3 / M4-C / M4-D / provider creation (per the brief: 'Do not begin the following task'). Two documented deviations: (1) The Capabilities_Resolved_Through_Service architecture test is deferred to M4-B.3 (the test asserts Diagnostics.razor contains @inject IHostCapabilitiesService; Diagnostics.razor does not exist in M4-B.1 — creating a fake file to make the test pass would be scope creep). (2) The DetectedAt test was split into two tests: (a) DetectedAt equals the TimeProvider's value (deterministic with FixedTimeProvider); (b) DetectedAt falls within the call window when the default clock is used (non-deterministic but bounded).

tasks.notes: M4-B.2 first session delivered 2026-07-13. Boundary slice: the AppCapabilityList data-owning four-state design-system component (IReadOnlyList<HostCapability> Capabilities; AppStatusDot Success/Error; AppBadge 'Credential set' for CredentialAvailable=true; aria-live=polite on the populated list) + the AppKeyValueList data-owning four-state design-system component (IReadOnlyList<KeyValuePair<string, string>> Items; AppKeyValueListFormat enum Plain/Boolean/Code; definition-list semantics with <dl>/<dt>/<dd>; aria-live=polite) + the AppKeyValueListFormat enum in Enums.cs + the Diagnostics/_Imports.razor + 13 bUnit tests for AppCapabilityList + 15 bUnit tests for AppKeyValueList + the C-023 + C-024 capability records. Pre-M4-B.2 baseline was 343 passed; M4-B.2 raises the bar to 370 passed (99 unit + 259 component + 12 architecture), 0 failed, 9 skipped. Branch: feature/T-025-m4-b-2-capability-list-components. Commit: feat(m4-b.2): add AppCapabilityList + AppKeyValueList data-owning design-system components. Push is staged for push (not authorised in this session). The session does NOT begin M4-B.3 / M4-C / M4-D / provider creation (per the brief: 'Do not begin the following task'). One documented deviation: AppCapabilityList default Populated uses an AppStack of AppCard entries with the AppStatusDot in the header (not the AppBadge-style credential slot), and the AppKeyValueList default Populated uses a definition list (<dl>/<dt>/<dd>) with monospaced <code> elements for the Code format; the plan's intent (one card per capability with status dot + version + credential badge; one row per item with key left + value right) is preserved.

tasks.title: Theme toggle is not interactive in the running app (M2.4 follow-up bug)
tasks.links_to_capability: AppShellRegion / AppThemeToggle (shell/theme capability; the M2.1 AppShellRegion and the M2.3 AppThemeToggle form the shell/theme surface; the bug was in the interactive render seam, not in the JS contract)
tasks.fix_summary: Added @rendermode InteractiveServer to AppThemeToggle.razor. The layout itself remains static for streaming SSR (the layout's @Body is a RenderFragment delegate, which cannot be serialized across the SSR → interactive boundary; Blazor throws InvalidOperationException at request time if @rendermode is applied to a LayoutComponentBase). The toggle is the only interactive component in the layout's tree, so declaring the directive on the toggle itself is the minimum-blast-radius fix. The new AppLayout_ThemeToggleWiringTests bUnit test renders the AppLayout, finds the toggle inside the topbar, clicks it, and asserts appTheme.set is invoked; this proves the click handler is wired when the layout is rendered (not the toggle in isolation). The visual smoke test confirms every route returns 200 and the toggle's markup is present on every page.
tasks.notes: Reproduction (before fix): (1) dotnet run on http://localhost:5170. (2) Open the home page. (3) The theme toggle control is visible in the top bar's Trailing slot. (4) Click the toggle. Actual: the click does not change the document theme. Expected: the data-theme attribute changes immediately, persists across navigation, persists across refresh. After the fix, the click changes the document theme immediately, and the IIFE in App.razor persists the theme via localStorage 'app-theme'. Fixed in the M2.5 slice per the user's explicit opt-in. Branch: feature/m2-5-empty-routes-responsive-accessibility. Commit: feat(m2.5): add empty routes, responsive matrix, a11y audit, and theme toggle fix.
